JodaBeanUtils.equal(settlementDateOffset, other.settlementDateOffset);\n   451\t    }\n   452\t    return false;\n   453\t  }\n   454\t\n   455\t  @Override\n   456\t  public int hashCode() {\n   457\t    int hash = getClass().hashCode();\n   458\t    hash = hash * 31 + JodaBeanUtils.hashCode(buySell);\n   459\t    hash = hash * 31 + JodaBeanUtils.hashCode(cdsIndexId);\n   460\t    hash = hash 463\t    hash = hash * 31 + JodaBeanUtils.hashCode(paymentPeriods);\n   464\t    hash = hash * 31 + JodaBeanUtils.hashCode(protectionEndDate);\n   465\t    hash = hash * 31 + 56. str_replace_editor @ 2026-03-11T18:36:50.364877

Input

{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "# CdsTranche Implementation for OpenGamma Strata\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document describes the design and implementation of the `CdsTranche` product type for pricing synthetic CDO tranches in OpenGamma Strata. The implementation spans three modules \u2014 `product`, `pricer`, and `measure` \u2014 and strictly follows the Joda-Beans and Strata patterns established by the existing CDS index (`CdsIndex` / `CdsIndexTrade`) family.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture and Layering\n\nThe implementation mirrors the `CdsIndex` \u2192 `CdsIndexTrade` \u2192 `ResolvedCdsIndex` \u2192 `ResolvedCdsIndexTrade` \u2192 `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexProductPricer` \u2192 `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer` \u2192 `CdsIndexTradeCalculationFunction` chain, introducing a parallel tranche-specific chain:\n\n```\nCdsTranche            (product)\nCdsTrancheTrade       (trade wrapper)\n  \u2502\n  \u25bc  .resolve(ReferenceData)\nResolvedCdsTranche    (resolved product, IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer input)\nResolvedCdsTrancheTrade (resolved trade, IsdaCdsTranchePricer input)\n  \u2502\n  \u25bc  pricing\nIsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer   (delegates to IsdaCdsProductPricer)\nIsdaCdsTranchePricer          (trade-level: product PV + upfront fee)\n  \u2502\n  \u25bc  measures\nCdsTrancheMeasureCalculations\nCdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction\n```\n\n---\n\n## Files Created\n\n### Product Module\n\n#### `CdsTranche.java`\n- Immutable Joda-Bean representing the unresolved product.\n- Fields: `buySell`, `cdsIndexId`, `legalEntityIds`, `attachmentPoint`, `detachmentPoint`, `currency`, `notional`, `paymentSchedule`, `fixedRate`, `dayCount`, `paymentOnDefault`, `protectionStart`, `stepinDateOffset`, `settlementDateOffset`.\n- The `attachmentPoint` and `detachmentPoint` are validated to be in `[0, 1]` with `detachmentPoint > attachmentPoint`.\n- `resolve(ReferenceData)` constructs a `ResolvedCdsTranche` by expanding the `PeriodicSchedule` into `CreditCouponPaymentPeriod` instances via the same pattern as `CdsIndex.resolve()`.\n- Implements `Product` and `Resolvable<ResolvedCdsTranche>`.\n\n#### `CdsTrancheTrade.java`\n- Immutable Joda-Bean wrapping `CdsTranche` as a trade.\n- Fields: `info` (TradeInfo), `product` (CdsTranche), `upfrontFee` (optional Payment).\n- Implements `ProductTrade`, `ResolvableTrade<ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade>`.\n- `summarize()` returns a `PortfolioItemSummary` using `ProductType.of(\"CDS_TRANCHE\")`.\n\n#### `ResolvedCdsTranche.java`\n- Immutable Joda-Bean: resolved form with all fields expanded (payment periods computed, dates adjusted).\n- Fields match those of `ResolvedCdsIndex` plus `attachmentPoint` and `detachmentPoint`.\n- Key helpers: `getAccrualStartDate()`, `getAccrualEndDate()`, `getNotional()`, `getCurrency()`, `getFixedRate()`, `getTrancheWidth()` (= `detachmentPoint - attachmentPoint`), `calculateEffectiveStartDate()`, `calculateSettlementDateFromValuation()`, `findPeriod()`, `accruedYearFraction()`.\n- **Bridge method** `toResolvedCdsIndex()`: builds a `ResolvedCdsIndex` from the tranche's fields, allowing the tranche to delegate to existing index pricers without code duplication.\n- Implements `ResolvedProduct`.\n\n#### `ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade.java`\n- Immutable Joda-Bean: resolved trade form.\n- Fields: `info` (TradeInfo, defaults to `TradeInfo.empty()`), `product` (ResolvedCdsTranche), `upfrontFee` (optional Payment).\n- Implements `ResolvedTrade`.\n- Full Joda-Beans boilerplate: Meta inner class with hash-code switch dispatch, Builder.\n\n---\n\n### Pricer Module\n\n#### `IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer.java`\n- Wraps `IsdaCdsProductPricer` (the core ISDA single-name pricer).\n- **Key design decision**: rather than re-implementing the ISDA model mathematics, it converts `ResolvedCdsTranche \u2192 ResolvedCdsIndex \u2192 ResolvedCds` via the bridge methods, then delegates to `IsdaCdsProductPricer`. This is identical to how `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexProductPricer` delegates.\n- The tranche modifier is applied via `getTrancheWidth()` scaling:\n  - `price(...)` \u2192 `underlyingPricer.price(cds, ...) * trancheWidth`\n  - `presentValue(...)` \u2192 `signedNotional * trancheWidth * underlyingPrice`\n  - `presentValueSensitivity(...)` \u2192 `underlyingPricer.presentValueSensitivity(cds, ...).multipliedBy(trancheWidth)`\n  - `parSpread(...)` \u2192 tranche-independent, delegates directly (par spread is a spread per unit protection, the trancheWidth cancels)\n  - `expectedLoss(...)` \u2192 simplified homogeneous pool formula: `(1 - RR) * (1 - SP) * trancheWidth * notional`\n- Expiry guard: returns zero / `PointSensitivityBuilder.none()` when `protectionEndDate <= valuationDate`.\n\n#### `IsdaCdsTranchePricer.java`\n- Trade-level pricer: adds upfront fee handling on top of `IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer`.\n- Mirrors `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer` exactly.\n- `presentValue(...)`: uses `ratesProvider.getValuationDate()` as the reference date (valuation-date PV), then adds `DiscountingPaymentPricer.presentValue(upfront, creditDiscountFactors.toDiscountFactors())`.\n- `presentValueOnSettle(...)`: uses the settlement date as reference date (for clean price coherence).\n- `presentValueSensitivity(...)`: combines product sensitivity (at valuation date) with upfront fee sensitivity via `toDiscountFactors()`.\n- `presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(...)`: product sensitivity at settlement date only (no fee).\n- `parSpread(...)`, `expectedLoss(...)`: delegate to product pricer.\n\n---\n\n### Measure Module\n\n#### `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.java`\n- Package-private class: multi-scenario calculation methods, one per measure.\n- Follows `CdsIndexMeasureCalculations` pattern exactly.\n- Supported measures:\n  - `presentValue` \u2192 dirty PV across all scenarios\n  - `principal` \u2192 clean PV on settle date\n  - `unitPrice` \u2192 clean price (PV per unit notional) on settle date\n  - `pv01CalibratedSum` / `pv01CalibratedBucketed` \u2192 calibrated curve PV01\n  - `pv01MarketQuoteSum` / `pv01MarketQuoteBucketed` \u2192 market-quote PV01 via `MarketQuoteSensitivityCalculator`\n  - `ir01CalibratedParallel` / `ir01CalibratedBucketed` \u2192 discount curve IR01 (using settle sensitivity)\n  - `expectedLoss` \u2192 tranche expected loss\n\n#### `CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction.java`\n- Public `CalculationFunction<CdsTrancheTrade>` that the calc engine dispatches to.\n- Implements `targetType()`, `supportedMeasures()`, `identifier()`, `naturalCurrency()`, `requirements()`, `calculate()`.\n- `requirements()`: uses `CreditRatesMarketDataLookup.requirements(cdsIndexId, currency)` to declare data needs.\n- `calculate()`: resolves the trade once, builds scenario market data view, then dispatches each requested measure to a `SingleMeasureCalculation` lambda from the static `CALCULATORS` map.\n- Supports `Measures.RESOLVED_TARGET` for resolved trade retrieval.\n\n---\n\n## Design Decisions\n\n### 1. Delegation Pattern via `toResolvedCdsIndex()`\nThe tranche reuses `ResolvedCdsIndex.toSingleNameCds()` \u2192 `IsdaCdsProductPricer` via the bridge. This avoids duplicating the ISDA numerics (accrual on default, protection full, risky annuity) while correctly applying the `trancheWidth` scalar. The ISDA model's assumption of a flat credit curve applies equally well to the tranche under the homogeneous pool approximation.\n\n### 2. Tranche Width Scaling\nThe tranche modifies three quantities relative to the full index:\n- **Protection PV**: The tranche only covers losses between `[attachment, detachment]`, so the effective protection notional is `trancheWidth \u00d7 fullNotional`.\n- **Premium PV**: The coupon is also paid on `trancheWidth \u00d7 fullNotional` effective exposure.\n- **Sensitivity**: Scales linearly with `trancheWidth` since both legs scale uniformly.\n- **Par Spread**: Does NOT scale with `trancheWidth` \u2014 it is a rate (ratio of protection leg to risky annuity), not an absolute quantity.\n\n### 3. `expectedLoss()` Approximation\nThe simplified formula `EL = (1 - RR) \u00d7 (1 - SP) \u00d7 trancheWidth \u00d7 notional` is a first-order homogeneous pool approximation. A more accurate model would integrate the conditional loss distribution over `[attachment, detachment]`, but this would require a Gaussian copula or similar model that is outside the ISDA framework. The simple form is appropriate as a first implementation and matches the level of complexity visible in the existing codebase.\n\n### 4. Joda-Beans Boilerplate\nAll four beans are hand-written (no code generation) following the fully expanded pattern of `ResolvedCdsIndex.java` and `ResolvedCdsIndexTrade.java`:\n- `MetaBean.register(...)` in static initializer\n- `Meta` inner class with `DirectMetaProperty` for each field\n- Hash-code switch dispatch using precomputed Java `String.hashCode()` values\n- `Builder` inner class with typed fluent setters and `set(String, Object)` / `set(MetaProperty, Object)` overloads\n- `serialVersionUID = 1L`\n\n### 5. Upfront Fee Handling\nThe `upfrontFee` `Payment` uses `ratesProvider.discountFactors(currency).toDiscountFactors()` \u2014 not the `CreditDiscountFactors` directly \u2014 because `DiscountingPaymentPricer` requires the base `DiscountFactors` interface. This matches the pattern in `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer`.\n\n### 6. Expiry Guard\nBoth the product pricer and trade pricer short-circuit when `protectionEndDate <= valuationDate`, returning zero PV / zero sensitivity. This matches `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexProductPricer.isExpired(...)`.\n\n---\n\n## Measure Coherence\n\n| Measure | Reference Date | Price Type | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `PRESENT_VALUE` | Valuation date | Dirty | Standard MTM PV |\n| `PRINCIPAL` | Settlement date | Clean | Price \u00d7 notional, fees excluded |\n| `UNIT_PRICE` | Settlement date | Clean | Price per unit notional |\n| `PV01_*` | Valuation date | n/a | Rate curve sensitivities |\n| `IR01_*` | Settlement date | n/a | Discount curve only |\n| `EXPECTED_LOSS` | Protection end | n/a | Survival \u00d7 (1 - RR) \u00d7 width |\n\n---\n\n## Integration Points\n\n- `CreditRatesMarketDataLookup`: provides `LegalEntitySurvivalProbabilities`, `CreditDiscountFactors`, `RecoveryRates` keyed by `cdsIndexId`.\n- `CreditRatesScenarioMarketData`: wraps multi-scenario market data; `scenario(i).creditRatesProvider()` gives a single-scenario provider.\n- `IsdaCdsProductPricer`: provides `price()`, `presentValueSensitivity()`, `parSpread()`, `riskyAnnuity()`, `protectionFull()`, `recoveryRate()`.\n- `DiscountingPaymentPricer`: prices the optional upfront fee.\n- `MarketQuoteSensitivityCalculator`: transforms calibrated sensitivities to market-quote sensitivities.\n",
  "path": "/logs/agent/solution.md",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"File created successfully at: /logs/agent/solution.md"

69. think @ 2026-03-11T18:38:40.784305

Input

{
  "thought": "I notice an important detail: in `CdsIndexMeasureCalculations.expectedLoss()`, the single-scenario method does NOT take `refData` as a parameter. My `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.expectedLoss()` follows the same pattern - the single-scenario method takes `(trade, ratesProvider)` without `refData`.\n\nHowever, in the `CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction.SingleMeasureCalculation` interface, ALL methods take `(trade, marketData, refData)`. The `expectedLoss` method in `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations` is called as a lambda:\n```java\n.put(CreditMeasures.EXPECTED_LOSS, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::expectedLoss)\n```\n\nBut this references the multi-scenario method `expectedLoss(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData)` which has the right signature for the `SingleMeasureCalculation` functional interface. So that's fine - it ignores the `refData` parameter internally.\n\nWait, the `SingleMeasureCalculation` interface I defined takes `(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData)` and the multi-scenario `expectedLoss` method takes `(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData)`. That matches.\n\nNow let me check the `ir01CalibratedParallel` method in `CdsIndexMeasureCalculations` to see the exact pattern used there.\n"
}

Output

"Your thought has been logged."

73. think @ 2026-03-11T18:39:12.745437

Input

{
  "thought": "Now I can see the exact pattern for `ir01CalibratedParallel` and `ir01CalibratedBucketed` in `CdsIndexMeasureCalculations`:\n\n```java\nPointSensitivities pointSensitivity = tradePricer.presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\nCurrencyParameterSensitivity irSensitivity = ratesProvider.singleDiscountCurveParameterSensitivity(\n    pointSensitivity,\n    trade.getProduct().getCurrency());\nreturn irSensitivity.total().multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);  // for parallel\nreturn irSensitivity.multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);  // for bucketed\n```\n\nMy `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations` uses the same pattern correctly:\n```java\nPointSensitivities pointSensitivity = tradePricer.presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\nCurrencyParameterSensitivity irSensitivity = ratesProvider.singleDiscountCurveParameterSensitivity(\n    pointSensitivity,\n    trade.getProduct().getCurrency());\nreturn irSensitivity.total().multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);  // for parallel\nreturn irSensitivity.multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);  // for bucketed\n```\n\nGood, that matches exactly.\n\nHowever, I notice that `CdsIndexMeasureCalculations.ir01CalibratedParallel` and `ir01CalibratedBucketed` use `tradePricer.presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(trade, ratesProvider, refData)` which is a method on `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer`. My `IsdaCdsTranchePricer` has `presentValueOnSettleSensitivity` that delegates to the product pricer. Let me verify the signature.\n\nIn my `IsdaCdsTranchePricer`:\n```java\npublic PointSensitivities presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(\n    ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n    ReferenceData refData) {\n\n  ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n  LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n  return productPricer.presentValueSensitivity(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, refData).build();\n}\n```\n\nThat's correct. The `presentValueOnSettleSensitivity` in my pricer takes `(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesProvider, ReferenceData)` which is what `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations` calls via `tradePricer.presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(trade, ratesProvider, refData)`.\n\nEverything looks good. Let me now verify one more critical thing: the `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer.presentValueOnSettleSensitivity` method signature to make sure my pricer matches it.\n"
}

Output

"Your thought has been logged."

This pricer delegates to\n    26\t * {@link IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer} for product-level calculations and adds\n    27\t * trade-level adjustments such as upfront fee pricing.\n    28\t * <p>\n    29\t * The tranche present value is computed by scaling the underlying index's\n    30\t * protection and premium legs by the tranche factor (detachmentPoint - attachmentPoint).\n    31\t * The protection leg only covers losses within the tranche range.\n    32\t */\n    33\tpublic class IsdaCdsTranchePricer {\n    34\t\n    35\t  /**\n    36\t   * Default implementation.\n    37\t   */\n    38\t  public static final IsdaCdsTranchePricer DEFAULT = new IsdaCdsTranchePricer(AccrualOnDefaultFormula.ORIGINAL_ISDA);\n    39\t\n    40\t  /**\n    41\t   * The product pricer.\n    42\t   */\n    43\t  private final IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer productPricer;\n    44\t  /**\n    45\t   * The upfront fee pricer.\n    46\t   */\n    47\t  private final DiscountingPaymentPricer upfrontPricer;\n    48\t\n    49\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    50\t  /**\n    51\t   * Creates an instance using the default accrual-on-default formula.\n    52\t   */\n    53\t  public IsdaCdsTranchePricer() {\n    54\t    this.productPricer = IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer.DEFAULT;\n    55\t    this.upfrontPricer = DiscountingPaymentPricer.DEFAULT;\n    56\t  }\n    57\t\n    58\t  /**\n    59\t   * Creates an instance specifying the accrual-on-default formula.\n    60\t   *\n    61\t   * @param formula  the accrual-on-default formula\n    62\t   */\n    63\t  public IsdaCdsTranchePricer(AccrualOnDefaultFormula formula) {\n    64\t    this.productPricer = new IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer(formula);\n    65\t    this.upfrontPricer = DiscountingPaymentPricer.DEFAULT;\n    66\t  }\n    67\t\n    68\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    69\t  /**\n    70\t   * Gets the accrual-on-default formula used in this pricer.\n    71\t   *\n    72\t   * @return the formula\n    73\t   */\n    74\t  public AccrualOnDefaultFormula getAccrualOnDefaultFormula() {\n    75\t    return productPricer.getAccrualOnDefaultFormula();\n    76\t  }\n    77\t\n    78\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    79\t  /**\n    80\t   * Calculates the price of the tranche trade (present value per unit notional).\n    81\t   * <p>\n    82\t   * This method can calculate the clean or dirty price.\n    83\t   *\n    84\t   * @param trade  the trade\n    85\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n    86\t   * @param priceType  the price type\n    87\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n    88\t   * @return the price\n    89\t   */\n    90\t  public double price(\n    91\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    92\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n    93\t      PriceType priceType,\n    94\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n    95\t\n    96\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n    97\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n    98\t    return productPricer.price(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, priceType, refData);\n    99\t  }\n   100\t\n   101\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   102\t  /**\n   103\t   * Calculates the present value of the tranche trade.\n   104\t   * <p>\n   105\t   * The present value of the product is based on the valuation date.\n   106\t   * If calculating the clean value, the accrued interest is based on the step-in date.\n   107\t   *\n   108\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   109\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   110\t   * @param priceType  the price type\n   111\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   112\t   * @return the present value\n   113\t   */\n   114\t  public CurrencyAmount presentValue(\n   115\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   116\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   117\t      PriceType priceType,\n   118\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   119\t\n   120\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   121\t    CurrencyAmount pvProduct = productPricer.presentValue(\n   122\t        tranche, ratesProvider, ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), priceType, refData);\n   123\t    if (trade.getUpfrontFee().isPresent()) {\n   124\t      Payment upfrontFee = trade.getUpfrontFee().get();\n   125\t      CurrencyAmount pvFee = upfrontPricer.presentValue(\n   126\t          upfrontFee, ratesProvider.discountFactors(upfrontFee.getCurrency()).toDiscountFactors());\n   127\t      return pvProduct.plus(pvFee);\n   128\t    }\n   129\t    return pvProduct;\n   130\t  }\n   131\t\n   132\t  /**\n   133\t   * Calculates the present value of the tranche trade on the settlement date.\n   134\t   * <p>\n   135\t   * The present value of the trade on the cash settlement date.\n   136\t   *\n   137\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   138\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   139\t   * @param priceType  the price type\n   140\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   141\t   * @return the present value on settlement\n   142\t   */\n   143\t  public CurrencyAmount presentValueOnSettle(\n   144\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   145\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   146\t      PriceType priceType,\n   147\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   148\t\n   149\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   150\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   151\t    return productPricer.presentValue(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, priceType, refData);\n   152\t  }\n   153\t\n   154\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   155\t  /**\n   156\t   * Calculates the present value sensitivity of the tranche trade.\n   157\t   * <p>\n   158\t   * The present value sensitivity of the trade is the sensitivity of present value\n   159\t   * to the underlying curves.\n   160\t   *\n   161\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   162\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   163\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   164\t   * @return the present value sensitivity\n   165\t   */\n   166\t  public PointSensitivities presentValueSensitivity(\n   167\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   168\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   169\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   170\t\n   171\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   172\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   173\t    PointSensitivityBuilder pvSensiProduct =\n   174\t        productPricer.presentValueSensitivity(tranche, ratesProvider, ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), refData);\n   175\t    if (trade.getUpfrontFee().isPresent()) {\n   176\t      Payment upfrontFee = trade.getUpfrontFee().get();\n   177\t      PointSensitivityBuilder pvSensiFee =\n   178\t          upfrontPricer.presentValueSensitivity(\n   179\t              upfrontFee, ratesProvider.discountFactors(upfrontFee.getCurrency()).toDiscountFactors());\n   180\t      return pvSensiProduct.combinedWith(pvSensiFee).build();\n   181\t    }\n   182\t    return pvSensiProduct.build();\n   183\t  }\n   184\t\n   185\t  /**\n   186\t   * Calculates the present value sensitivity of the tranche trade on settlement.\n   187\t   *\n   188\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   189\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   190\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   191\t   * @return the present value on settlement sensitivity\n   192\t   */\n   193\t  public PointSensitivities presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(\n   194\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   195\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   196\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   197\t\n   198\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   199\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   200\t    return productPricer.presentValueSensitivity(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, refData).build();\n   201\t  }\n   202\t\n   203\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   204\t  /**\n   205\t   * Calculates the par spread of the tranche trade.\n   206\t   * <p>\n   207\t   * The par spread is the coupon rate that makes the clean PV equal to zero.\n   208\t   *\n   209\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   210\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   211\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   212\t   * @return the par spread\n   213\t   */\n   214\t  public double parSpread(\n   215\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   216\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   217\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   218\t\n   219\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   220\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   221\t    return productPricer.parSpread(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, refData);\n   222\t  }\n   223\t\n   224\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   225\t  /**\n   226\t   * Calculates the expected loss of the tranche.\n   227\t   * <p>\n   228\t   * The expected loss represents the expected credit loss for the tranche,\n   229\t   * computed as the expected portfolio loss that falls within the tranche boundaries.\n   230\t   *\n   231\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   232\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   233\t   * @return the expected loss\n   234\t   */\n   235\t  public CurrencyAmount expectedLoss(\n   236\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   237\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider) {\n   238\t\n   239\t    return productPricer.expectedLoss(trade.getProduct(), ratesProvider);\n   240\t  }\n   241\t\n   242\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   243\t  // calculate the settlement date\n   244\t  private LocalDate calculateSettlementDate(\n   245\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   246\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   247\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   248\t\n   249\t    return trade.getProduct().calculateSettlementDateFromValuation(ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), refData);\n   250\t  }\n   251\t\n   252\t}\n   253\t\n"

com.opengamma.strata.product.credit.ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade;\n    25\t\n    26\t/**\n    27\t * Multi-scenario measure calculations for CDS tranche trades.\n    28\t * <p>\n    29\t * Each method corresponds to a measure, typically calculated by one or more calls to the pricer.\n    30\t */\n    31\tfinal class CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations {\n    32\t\n    33\t  /**\n    34\t   * Default implementation.\n    35\t   */\n    36\t  public static final CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations DEFAULT =\n    37\t      new CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations(new IsdaCdsTranchePricer(AccrualOnDefaultFormula.CORRECT));\n    38\t\n    39\t  /**\n    40\t   * The market quote sensitivity calculator.\n    41\t   */\n    42\t  private static final MarketQuoteSensitivityCalculator MARKET_QUOTE_SENS = MarketQuoteSensitivityCalculator.DEFAULT;\n    43\t  /**\n    44\t   * One basis point, expressed as a {@code double}.\n    45\t   */\n    46\t  private static final double ONE_BASIS_POINT = 1e-4;\n    47\t\n    48\t  /**\n    49\t   * Pricer for {@link ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade}.\n    50\t   */\n    51\t  private final IsdaCdsTranchePricer tradePricer;\n    52\t\n    53\t  /**\n    54\t   * Creates an instance.\n    55\t   *\n    56\t   * @param tradePricer  the pricer for {@link ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade}\n    57\t   */\n    58\t  public CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations(IsdaCdsTranchePricer tradePricer) {\n    59\t    this.tradePricer = ArgChecker.notNull(tradePricer, \"tradePricer\");\n    60\t  }\n    61\t\n    62\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    63\t  // calculates present value for all scenarios\n    64\t  CurrencyScenarioArray presentValue(\n    65\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    66\t      CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n    67\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n    68\t\n    69\t    return CurrencyScenarioArray.of(\n    70\t        marketData.getScenarioCount(),\n    71\t        i -> presentValue(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider(), PriceType.DIRTY, refData));\n    72\t  }\n    73\t\n    74\t  // calculates present value for one scenario\n    75\t  CurrencyAmount presentValue(\n    76\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    77\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n    78\t      PriceType priceType,\n    79\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n    80\t\n    81\t    return tradePricer.presentValue(trade, ratesProvider, priceType, refData);\n    82\t  }\n    83\t\n    84\t scenario\n    97\t  CurrencyAmount principal(\n    98\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    99\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   100\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   101\t\n   102\t    return tradePricer.presentValueOnSettle(trade, ratesProvider, PriceType.CLEAN, refData);\n   103\t  }\n   104\t\n   105\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   106\t  // calculates unit price for all scenarios\n   107\t  DoubleScenarioArray unitPrice(\n   108\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   109\t      CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n   110\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   111\t\n   112\t    return DoubleScenarioArray.of(\n   113\t scenario\n   118\t  double unitPrice(\n   119\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   120\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   121\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   122\t\n   123\t    return tradePricer.price(trade, ratesProvider, PriceType.CLEAN, refData);\n   124\t  }\n   125\t\n   126\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   127\t  // calculates calibrated sum PV01 for all scenarios\n   128\t  MultiCurrencyScenarioArray pv01CalibratedSum(\n   129\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   130\t      CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n   131\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   132\t\n   133\t    return MultiCurrencyScenarioArray.of(\n   134\t        marketData.getScenarioCount(),\n   135\t        i -> pv01CalibratedSum(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider(), refData));\n   136\t  }\n   137\t\n   138\t  // calculates calibrated sum PV01 for one scenario\n   139\t  MultiCurrencyAmount pv01CalibratedSum(\n   140\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   141\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   142\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   143\t\n   144\t    PointSensitivities pointSensitivity = tradePricer.presentValueSensitivity(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   145\t    return ratesProvider.parameterSensitivity(pointSensitivity).total().multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);\n   146\t  }\n   147\t\n   148\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   149\t  // calculates calibrated bucketed PV01 for all scenarios\n   150\t pv01CalibratedBucketed(\n   162\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   163\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   164\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   165\t\n   166\t ratesProvider.parameterSensitivity(pointSensitivity).multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);\n   168\t  }\n   169\t\n   170\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   171\t  // calculates market quote sum PV01 for all scenarios\n   172\t  MultiCurrencyScenarioArray pv01MarketQuoteSum(\n   173\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   174\t        i -> pv01MarketQuoteSum(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider(), refData));\n   180\t  }\n   181\t\n   182\t  // calculates market quote sum PV01 for one scenario\n   183\t ratesProvider.parameterSensitivity(pointSensitivity);\n   190\t    CurrencyParameterSensitivities quoteSensitivity = MARKET_QUOTE_SENS.sensitivity(parameterSensitivity, ratesProvider);\n   191\t    return quoteSensitivity.total().multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);\n   192\t  }\n   193\t\n   194\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   195\t  // calculates market quote bucketed PV01 for all scenarios\n   196\t  ScenarioArray<CurrencyParameterSensitivities> pv01MarketQuoteBucketed(\n   197\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   198\t pv01MarketQuoteBucketed(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider(), refData));\n   204\t  }\n   205\t\n   206\t  // calculates market quote bucketed PV01 for one scenario\n   207\t  CurrencyParameterSensitivities pv01MarketQuoteBucketed(\n   208\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   209\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   210\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   211\t\n   212\t    PointSensitivities pointSensitivity = tradePricer.presentValueSensitivity(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   213\t    CurrencyParameterSensitivities parameterSensitivity = ratesProvider.parameterSensitivity(pointSensitivity);\n   214\t    return MARKET_QUOTE_SENS.sensitivity(parameterSensitivity, ratesProvider).multipliedBy(ONE_BASIS_POINT);\n   215\t  }\n   216\t\n   217\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   218\t  // calculates calibrated parallel IR01 for all scenarios\n   219\t  CurrencyScenarioArray ir01CalibratedParallel(\n   220\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   221\t      CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n   222\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   223\t\n   224\t    return com.opengamma.strata.product.credit.ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade;\n    29\t\n    30\t/**\n    31\t * Perform calculations on a single {@code CdsTrancheTrade} for each of a set of scenarios.\n    32\t * <p>\n    33\t * An instance of {@link CreditRatesMarketDataLookup} must be specified.\n    34\t * The supported built-in measures are:\n    35\t * <ul>\n    36\t *   <li>{@linkplain Measures#PRESENT_VALUE Present value}\n    37\t *   <li>{@linkplain Measures#PV01_CALIBRATED_SUM PV01 calibrated sum on rate curves}\n    38\t *   <li>{@linkplain Measures#PV01_CALIBRATED_BUCKETED PV01 calibrated bucketed on rate curves}\n    39\t *   <li>{@linkplain Measures#PV01_MARKET_QUOTE_SUM PV01 market quote sum on rate curves}\n    40\t *   <li>{@linkplain Measures#PV01_MARKET_QUOTE_BUCKETED PV01 market quote bucketed on rate curves}\n    41\t *   <li>{@linkplain Measures#UNIT_PRICE Unit price}\n    42\t *   <li>{@linkplain CreditMeasures#PRINCIPAL principal}\n    43\t *   <li>{@linkplain CreditMeasures#IR01_CALIBRATED_PARALLEL IR01 calibrated parallel}\n    44\t *   <li>{@linkplain CreditMeasures#IR01_CALIBRATED_BUCKETED IR01 calibrated bucketed}\n    45\t *   <li>{@linkplain CreditMeasures#EXPECTED_LOSS expected loss}\n    46\t * </ul>\n    47\t * <p>\n    48\t * The \"natural\" currency is the currency of the CDS tranche, which is limited to be single-currency.\n    49\t */\n    50\tpublic class CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction\n    51\t    implements CalculationFunction<CdsTrancheTrade> {\n    52\t\n    53\t  /**\n    54\t   * The calculations by measure.\n    55\t   */\n    56\t  private static final ImmutableMap<Measure, SingleMeasureCalculation> CALCULATORS =\n    57\t      ImmutableMap.<Measure, SingleMeasureCalculation>builder()\n    58\t          .put(Measures.PRESENT_VALUE, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::presentValue)\n    59\t          .put(Measures.PV01_CALIBRATED_SUM, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::pv01CalibratedSum)\n    60\t          .put(Measures.PV01_CALIBRATED_BUCKETED, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::pv01CalibratedBucketed)\n    61\t          .put(Measures.PV01_MARKET_QUOTE_SUM, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::pv01MarketQuoteSum)\n    62\t          .put(Measures.PV01_MARKET_QUOTE_BUCKETED, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::pv01MarketQuoteBucketed)\n    63\t          .put(Measures.UNIT_PRICE, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::unitPrice)\n    64\t          .put(CreditMeasures.PRINCIPAL, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::principal)\n    65\t          .put(CreditMeasures.IR01_CALIBRATED_PARALLEL, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::ir01CalibratedParallel)\n    66\t          .put(CreditMeasures.IR01_CALIBRATED_BUCKETED, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::ir01CalibratedBucketed)\n    67\t          .put(CreditMeasures.EXPECTED_LOSS, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::expectedLoss)\n    68\t          .put(Measures.RESOLVED_TARGET, (rt, smd, rd) -> rt)\n    69\t          .build();\n    70\t\n    71\t  private static final ImmutableSet<Measure> MEASURES = CALCULATORS.keySet();\n    72\t\n    73\t  /**\n    74\t   * Creates an instance.\n    75\t   */\n    76\t  public CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction() {\n    77\t  }\n    78\t\n    79\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    80\t  @Override\n    81\t  public Class<CdsTrancheTrade> targetType() {\n    82\t    return CdsTrancheTrade.class;\n    83\t  }\n    84\t\n    85\t  @Override\n    86\t  public Set<Measure> supportedMeasures() {\n    87\t    return MEASURES;\n    88\t  }\n    89\t\n    90\t  @Override\n    91\t  public Optional<String> identifier(CdsTrancheTrade target) {\n    92\t    return target.getInfo().getId().map(id -> id.toString());\n    93\t  }\n    94\t\n    95\t  @Override\n    96\t  public Currency naturalCurrency(CdsTrancheTrade trade, ReferenceData refData) {\n    97\t    return trade.getProduct().getCurrency();\n    98\t  }\n    99\t\n   100\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   101\t  @Override\n   102\t  public FunctionRequirements requirements(\n   103\t      CdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   104\t      Set<Measure> measures,\n   105\t      CalculationParameters parameters,\n   106\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   107\t\n   108\t    // extract data from product\n   109\t    CdsTranche product = trade.getProduct();\n   110\t    StandardId legalEntityId = product.getCdsIndexId();\n   111\t    Currency currency = product.getCurrency();\n   112\t\n   113\t    // use lookup to build requirements\n   114\t    CreditRatesMarketDataLookup lookup = parameters.getParameter(CreditRatesMarketDataLookup.class);\n   115\t    return lookup.requirements(legalEntityId, currency);\n   116\t  }\n   117\t\n   118\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   119\t  @Override\n   120\t  public Map<Measure, Result<?>> calculate(\n   121\t      CdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   122\t      Set<Measure> measures,\n   123\t      CalculationParameters parameters,\n   124\t      ScenarioMarketData scenarioMarketData,\n   125\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   126\t\n   127\t    // resolve the trade once for all measures and all scenarios\n   128\t    ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade resolved = trade.resolve(refData);\n   129\t\n   130\t    // use lookup to query market data\n   131\t    CreditRatesMarketDataLookup ledLookup = parameters.getParameter(CreditRatesMarketDataLookup.class);\n   132\t    CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData = ledLookup.marketDataView(scenarioMarketData);\n   133\t\n   134\t    // loop around measures, calculating all scenarios for one measure\n   135\t    Map<Measure, Result<?>> results = new HashMap<>();\n   136\t    for (Measure measure : measures) {\n   137\t      results.put(measure, calculate(measure, resolved, marketData, refData));\n   138\t    }\n   139\t    return results;\n   140\t  }\n   141\t\n   142\t  // calculate one measure\n   143\t  private Result<?> calculate(\n   144\t      Measure measure,\n   145\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   146\t      CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n   147\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   148\t\n   149\t    SingleMeasureCalculation calculator = CALCULATORS.get(measure);\n   150\t    if (calculator == null) {\n   151\t      return Result.failure(FailureReason.UNSUPPORTED, \"Unsupported measure for CdsTrancheTrade: {}\", measure);\n   152\t    }\n   153\t    return Result.of(() -> calculator.calculate(trade, marketData, refData));\n   154\t  }\n   155\t\n   156\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   157\t  @FunctionalInterface\n   158\t  interface SingleMeasureCalculation {\n   159\t    public abstract Object calculate(\n   160\t        ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   161\t        CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n   162\t        ReferenceData refData);\n   163\t  }\n   164\t\n   165\t}\n   166\t\n"

{
  "thought": "Looking at the `CdsIndexMeasureCalculations.expectedLoss()` multi-scenario method:\n\n```java\nCurrencyScenarioArray expectedLoss(\n    ResolvedCdsIndexTrade trade,\n    CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n    ReferenceData refData) {\n\n  return CurrencyScenarioArray.of(\n      marketData.getScenarioCount(),\n      i -> expectedLoss(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider()));\n}\n```\n\nThe lambda `i -> expectedLoss(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider())` calls the single-scenario method WITHOUT `refData`. This is fine because `expectedLoss` on the pricer doesn't need `refData`.\n\nIn our `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations`, we have exactly the same:\n```java\nCurrencyScenarioArray expectedLoss(\n    ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    CreditRatesScenarioMarketData marketData,\n    ReferenceData refData) {\n\n  return CurrencyScenarioArray.of(\n      marketData.getScenarioCount(),\n      i -> expectedLoss(trade, marketData.scenario(i).creditRatesProvider()));\n}\n```\n\nThe multi-scenario `expectedLoss` method in `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations` has the signature `(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData)` which matches the `SingleMeasureCalculation` functional interface. The lambda reference `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::expectedLoss` in `CALCULATORS` refers to the multi-scenario overload, which is correct.\n\nHowever! I notice a potential issue: there are two `expectedLoss` methods in `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations`:\n1. `CurrencyScenarioArray expectedLoss(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData)` - multi-scenario, matches `SingleMeasureCalculation`  \n2. `CurrencyAmount expectedLoss(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesProvider)` - single-scenario\n\nWhen Java resolves `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::expectedLoss` against the `SingleMeasureCalculation` interface with signature `(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData) -> Object`, it will correctly select method (1) because (2) has a different parameter signature.\n\nSo there's no issue here. \n\nNow let me check: is there an issue with the `presentValue` method reference? In `CALCULATORS`:\n```java\n.put(Measures.PRESENT_VALUE, CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.DEFAULT::presentValue)\n```\n\n`SingleMeasureCalculation.calculate` expects `(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData) -> Object`.\n\nIn `CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations`, there are two `presentValue` methods:\n1. `CurrencyScenarioArray presentValue(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesScenarioMarketData, ReferenceData)` - matches `SingleMeasureCalculation`\n2. `CurrencyAmount presentValue(ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade, CreditRatesProvider, PriceType, ReferenceData)` - does NOT match (4 params)\n\nJava will select (1) as the target method for the method reference. \u2713\n\nEverything looks correct. Let me also double-check the `IsdaCdsTranchePricer.presentValueSensitivity` variable `settlementDate` which is computed but not used:\n\nLine 172: `LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);`\nLine 173-174: uses `ratesProvider.getValuationDate()` instead\n\nThat's a dead variable. Let me fix it.\n\nActually wait - looking more carefully at line 172 in `IsdaCdsTranchePricer.java`:\n```java\nLocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\nPointSensitivityBuilder pvSensiProduct =\n    productPricer.presentValueSensitivity(tranche, ratesProvider, ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), refData);\n```\n\n`settlementDate` is computed but not used in `presentValueSensitivity`. That's indeed a dead variable warning. I should remove it.\n\nLet me also check `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer.presentValueSensitivity` to see whether it uses valuation date or settlement date for computing `pvSensiProduct`.\n"
}

This pricer delegates to\n    26\t * {@link IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer} for product-level calculations and adds\n    27\t * trade-level adjustments such as upfront fee pricing.\n    28\t * <p>\n    29\t * The tranche present value is computed by scaling the underlying index's\n    30\t * protection and premium legs by the tranche factor (detachmentPoint - attachmentPoint).\n    31\t * The protection leg only covers losses within the tranche range.\n    32\t */\n    33\tpublic class IsdaCdsTranchePricer {\n    34\t\n    35\t  /**\n    36\t   * Default implementation.\n    37\t   */\n    38\t  public static final IsdaCdsTranchePricer DEFAULT = new IsdaCdsTranchePricer(AccrualOnDefaultFormula.ORIGINAL_ISDA);\n    39\t\n    40\t  /**\n    41\t   * The product pricer.\n    42\t   */\n    43\t  private final IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer productPricer;\n    44\t  /**\n    45\t   * The upfront fee pricer.\n    46\t   */\n    47\t  private final DiscountingPaymentPricer upfrontPricer;\n    48\t\n    49\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    50\t  /**\n    51\t   * Creates an instance using the default accrual-on-default formula.\n    52\t   */\n    53\t  public IsdaCdsTranchePricer() {\n    54\t    this.productPricer = IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer.DEFAULT;\n    55\t    this.upfrontPricer = DiscountingPaymentPricer.DEFAULT;\n    56\t  }\n    57\t\n    58\t  /**\n    59\t   * Creates an instance specifying the accrual-on-default formula.\n    60\t   *\n    61\t   * @param formula  the accrual-on-default formula\n    62\t   */\n    63\t  public IsdaCdsTranchePricer(AccrualOnDefaultFormula formula) {\n    64\t    this.productPricer = new IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer(formula);\n    65\t    this.upfrontPricer = DiscountingPaymentPricer.DEFAULT;\n    66\t  }\n    67\t\n    68\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    69\t  /**\n    70\t   * Gets the accrual-on-default formula used in this pricer.\n    71\t   *\n    72\t   * @return the formula\n    73\t   */\n    74\t  public AccrualOnDefaultFormula getAccrualOnDefaultFormula() {\n    75\t    return productPricer.getAccrualOnDefaultFormula();\n    76\t  }\n    77\t\n    78\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    79\t  /**\n    80\t   * Calculates the price of the tranche trade (present value per unit notional).\n    81\t   * <p>\n    82\t   * This method can calculate the clean or dirty price.\n    83\t   *\n    84\t   * @param trade  the trade\n    85\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n    86\t   * @param priceType  the price type\n    87\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n    88\t   * @return the price\n    89\t   */\n    90\t  public double price(\n    91\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n    92\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n    93\t      PriceType priceType,\n    94\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n    95\t\n    96\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n    97\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n    98\t    return productPricer.price(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, priceType, refData);\n    99\t  }\n   100\t\n   101\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   102\t  /**\n   103\t   * Calculates the present value of the tranche trade.\n   104\t   * <p>\n   105\t   * The present value of the product is based on the valuation date.\n   106\t   * If calculating the clean value, the accrued interest is based on the step-in date.\n   107\t   *\n   108\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   109\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   110\t   * @param priceType  the price type\n   111\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   112\t   * @return the present value\n   113\t   */\n   114\t  public CurrencyAmount presentValue(\n   115\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   116\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   117\t      PriceType priceType,\n   118\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   119\t\n   120\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   121\t    CurrencyAmount pvProduct = productPricer.presentValue(\n   122\t        tranche, ratesProvider, ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), priceType, refData);\n   123\t    if (trade.getUpfrontFee().isPresent()) {\n   124\t      Payment upfrontFee = trade.getUpfrontFee().get();\n   125\t      CurrencyAmount pvFee = upfrontPricer.presentValue(\n   126\t          upfrontFee, ratesProvider.discountFactors(upfrontFee.getCurrency()).toDiscountFactors());\n   127\t      return pvProduct.plus(pvFee);\n   128\t    }\n   129\t    return pvProduct;\n   130\t  }\n   131\t\n   132\t  /**\n   133\t   * Calculates the present value of the tranche trade on the settlement date.\n   134\t   * <p>\n   135\t   * The present value of the trade on the cash settlement date.\n   136\t   *\n   137\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   138\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   139\t   * @param priceType  the price type\n   140\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   141\t   * @return the present value on settlement\n   142\t   */\n   143\t  public CurrencyAmount presentValueOnSettle(\n   144\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   145\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   146\t      PriceType priceType,\n   147\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   148\t\n   149\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   150\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   151\t    return productPricer.presentValue(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, priceType, refData);\n   152\t  }\n   153\t\n   154\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   155\t  /**\n   156\t   * Calculates the present value sensitivity of the tranche trade.\n   157\t   * <p>\n   158\t   * The present value sensitivity of the trade is the sensitivity of present value\n   159\t   * to the underlying curves.\n   160\t   *\n   161\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   162\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   163\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   164\t   * @return the present value sensitivity\n   165\t   */\n   166\t  public PointSensitivities presentValueSensitivity(\n   167\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   168\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   169\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   170\t\n   171\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   172\t    PointSensitivityBuilder pvSensiProduct =\n   173\t        productPricer.presentValueSensitivity(tranche, ratesProvider, ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), refData);\n   174\t    if (trade.getUpfrontFee().isPresent()) {\n   175\t      Payment upfrontFee = trade.getUpfrontFee().get();\n   176\t      PointSensitivityBuilder pvSensiFee =\n   177\t          upfrontPricer.presentValueSensitivity(\n   178\t              upfrontFee, ratesProvider.discountFactors(upfrontFee.getCurrency()).toDiscountFactors());\n   179\t      return pvSensiProduct.combinedWith(pvSensiFee).build();\n   180\t    }\n   181\t    return pvSensiProduct.build();\n   182\t  }\n   183\t\n   184\t  /**\n   185\t   * Calculates the present value sensitivity of the tranche trade on settlement.\n   186\t   *\n   187\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   188\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   189\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   190\t   * @return the present value on settlement sensitivity\n   191\t   */\n   192\t  public PointSensitivities presentValueOnSettleSensitivity(\n   193\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   194\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   195\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   196\t\n   197\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   198\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   199\t    return productPricer.presentValueSensitivity(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, refData).build();\n   200\t  }\n   201\t\n   202\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   203\t  /**\n   204\t   * Calculates the par spread of the tranche trade.\n   205\t   * <p>\n   206\t   * The par spread is the coupon rate that makes the clean PV equal to zero.\n   207\t   *\n   208\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   209\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   210\t   * @param refData  the reference data\n   211\t   * @return the par spread\n   212\t   */\n   213\t  public double parSpread(\n   214\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   215\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   216\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   217\t\n   218\t    ResolvedCdsTranche tranche = trade.getProduct();\n   219\t    LocalDate settlementDate = calculateSettlementDate(trade, ratesProvider, refData);\n   220\t    return productPricer.parSpread(tranche, ratesProvider, settlementDate, refData);\n   221\t  }\n   222\t\n   223\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   224\t  /**\n   225\t   * Calculates the expected loss of the tranche.\n   226\t   * <p>\n   227\t   * The expected loss represents the expected credit loss for the tranche,\n   228\t   * computed as the expected portfolio loss that falls within the tranche boundaries.\n   229\t   *\n   230\t   * @param trade  the trade\n   231\t   * @param ratesProvider  the rates provider\n   232\t   * @return the expected loss\n   233\t   */\n   234\t  public CurrencyAmount expectedLoss(\n   235\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   236\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider) {\n   237\t\n   238\t    return productPricer.expectedLoss(trade.getProduct(), ratesProvider);\n   239\t  }\n   240\t\n   241\t  //-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   242\t  // calculate the settlement date\n   243\t  private LocalDate calculateSettlementDate(\n   244\t      ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade trade,\n   245\t      CreditRatesProvider ratesProvider,\n   246\t      ReferenceData refData) {\n   247\t\n   248\t    return trade.getInfo().getSettlementDate()\n   249\t        .orElse(trade.getProduct().calculateSettlementDateFromValuation(ratesProvider.getValuationDate(), refData));\n   250\t  }\n   251\t\n   252\t}\n   253\t\n"

Input

{
  "command": "GIT_DIR=/workspace/.git GIT_WORK_TREE=/workspace \\\n  git commit -m \"feat: Implement CdsTranche product type for synthetic CDO tranche pricing\n\nAdd CdsTranche product type following Joda-Beans patterns across the\nproduct, pricer, and measure modules, mirroring the CdsIndex family.\n\nProduct module (4 files):\n- CdsTranche: unresolved product bean with attachmentPoint/detachmentPoint\n  in [0,1], validated that detachmentPoint > attachmentPoint; resolves\n  via PeriodicSchedule expansion into CreditCouponPaymentPeriod list\n- CdsTrancheTrade: trade wrapper with optional upfrontFee Payment\n- ResolvedCdsTranche: resolved form with getTrancheWidth() helper and\n  toResolvedCdsIndex() bridge enabling delegation to existing pricers\n- ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade: resolved trade bean\n\nPricer module (2 files):\n- IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer: delegates to IsdaCdsProductPricer via\n  toResolvedCdsIndex().toSingleNameCds(); scales price/PV by trancheWidth,\n  scales sensitivity via .multipliedBy(trancheWidth); parSpread is\n  unscaled (rate cancels); expectedLoss uses homogeneous pool formula\n- IsdaCdsTranchePricer: trade-level pricer mirroring\n  IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer; presentValue uses getValuationDate(),\n  presentValueOnSettle uses settlement date; upfront fee discounted via\n  .toDiscountFactors() on CreditDiscountFactors; calculateSettlementDate\n  prefers trade.getInfo().getSettlementDate() then falls back to product\n\nMeasure module (2 files):\n- CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations: multi-scenario calculations for\n  PRESENT_VALUE, PRINCIPAL, UNIT_PRICE, PV01_CALIBRATED_{SUM,BUCKETED},\n  PV01_MARKET_QUOTE_{SUM,BUCKETED}, IR01_CALIBRATED_{PARALLEL,BUCKETED},\n  EXPECTED_LOSS\n- CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction: CalculationFunction<CdsTrancheTrade>\n  using CreditRatesMarketDataLookup; resolves trade once, dispatches\n  each measure through CALCULATORS map; supports RESOLVED_TARGET\n\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>\"",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

resolves\n  via PeriodicSchedule expansion into CreditCouponPaymentPeriod list\n- CdsTrancheTrade: trade wrapper with optional upfrontFee Payment\n- ResolvedCdsTranche: resolved form with getTrancheWidth() helper and\n  toResolvedCdsIndex() bridge enabling delegation to existing pricers\n- ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade: resolved trade bean\nPricer module (2 files):\n- IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer: delegates to IsdaCdsProductPricer via\n  toResolvedCdsIndex().toSingleNameCds(); scales price/PV by trancheWidth,\n  scales sensitivity via .multipliedBy(trancheWidth); parSpread is\n  unscaled (rate cancels); expectedLoss uses homogeneous pool formula\n- IsdaCdsTranchePricer: trade-level pricer mirroring\n  IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer; presentValue uses getValuationDate(),\n  presentValueOnSettle uses settlement date; upfront fee discounted via\n  .toDiscountFactors() on CreditDiscountFactors; calculateSettlementDate\n  prefers trade.getInfo().getSettlementDate() then falls back to product\nMeasure module (2 files):\n- CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations: multi-scenario calculations for\n  PRESENT_VALUE, PRINCIPAL, UNIT_PRICE, PV01_CALIBRATED_{SUM,BUCKETED},\n  PV01_MARKET_QUOTE_{SUM,BUCKETED}, IR01_CALIBRATED_{PARALLEL,BUCKETED},\n  EXPECTED_LOSS\n- CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction: CalculationFunction<CdsTrancheTrade>\n  using CreditRatesMarketDataLookup; resolves trade once, dispatches\n  each measure through CALCULATORS map; supports RESOLVED_TARGET\nCo-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>\"\n[master (root-commit) be68ee3] feat: Implement CdsTranche product type for synthetic CDO tranche pricing\n 8 files changed, 3936 insertions(+)\n create mode 100644 modules/measure/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/measure/credit/CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.java\n create mode 100644 modules/measure/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/measure/credit/CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction.java\n create mode 100644 modules/pricer/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/pricer/credit/IsdaCdsTranchePricer.java\n create mode 100644 modules/pricer/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/pricer/credit/IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer.java\n create mode 100644 modules/product/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/product/credit/CdsTranche.java\n create mode 100644 modules/product/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/product/credit/CdsTrancheTrade.java\n create mode 100644 modules/product/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/product/credit/ResolvedCdsTranche.java\n create mode 100644 modules/product/src/main/java/com/opengamma/strata/product/credit/ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade.java"

"# Task List\n\n1. \u2705 Explore repository structure and existing CDS/CdsIndex patterns\n\n2. \u2705 Create CdsTranche.java product class\n\n3. \u2705 Create CdsTrancheTrade.java trade wrapper\n\n4. \u2705 Create ResolvedCdsTranche.java resolved form\n\n5. \u2705 Create ResolvedCdsTrancheTrade.java resolved trade\n\n6. \u2705 Create IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer.java and IsdaCdsTranchePricer.java\n\n7. \u2705 Create CdsTrancheMeasureCalculations.java and CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction.java\n\n8. \u2705 Write solution.md analysis document\n\n"
99. finish @ 2026-03-11T18:42:34.994228

Input

{
  "message": "The CdsTranche implementation is complete and committed (commit `be68ee3`). All 8 files are in place across the product, pricer, and measure modules, following Joda-Beans patterns consistently with the CdsIndex family.\n\n**Final corrections applied:**\n1. Removed dead `settlementDate` variable from `IsdaCdsTranchePricer.presentValueSensitivity` \u2014 matching `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer` which uses `ratesProvider.getValuationDate()` directly\n2. Fixed `calculateSettlementDate` to prefer `trade.getInfo().getSettlementDate()` first, then fall back to `calculateSettlementDateFromValuation` \u2014 exactly matching the index pricer\n3. Removed unused `ArgChecker` import from `IsdaCdsTranchePricer`\n\n**Architecture summary:**\n- `ResolvedCdsTranche.toResolvedCdsIndex()` provides the bridge to existing ISDA numerics\n- `IsdaCdsTrancheProductPricer` delegates via `toResolvedCdsIndex().toSingleNameCds()` \u2192 `IsdaCdsProductPricer`, scaling by `trancheWidth`\n- `IsdaCdsTranchePricer` mirrors `IsdaHomogenousCdsIndexTradePricer` exactly for upfront fee handling and settlement date resolution\n- `CdsTrancheTradeCalculationFunction` supports 10 measures plus `RESOLVED_TARGET`, using `CreditRatesMarketDataLookup` for market data"
}

No code changes parsed.
